figures.text: Figure 1. Indicator empirical variogram used to select the spatial block distance. The selected block distance (approximately 151 km) was used to construct the zero-leakage spatial folds and the spatially separated conformal calibration and test partitions.
Figure 2. Spatial cross-fitted classifier performance and risk-controlled router decisions.
Figure 3. Field-verification budget curves under spatial evaluation.

figures.label: S1
figures.text: Figure S1. Open public ground-view availability by queue. The zero-found result is retained because it is the computed outcome of the open-evidence search.

figures.label: S2
figures.text: Figure S2. Public evidence query status. This figure documents search status rather than in-situ validation.

figures.label: S3
figures.text: Figure S3. Weak-reference contamination against verified smallholder labels.
Figure 4. AoA and conformal routing diagnostics. AoA is a safety gate; it is not reported as a proven singleton-error enrichment effect in the current sample set.
Figure 5. Leave-region feasibility audit under zero-leakage constraints.
Figure 6. Spatial-block sensitivity and effective spatial folds.
